name: GPT-5 Pro
archDisplay: Unified reasoning system behind GPT-5's auto-router; dense transformer, exact size undisclosed; top extended-reasoning tier defaulting to maximum reasoning effort
note: Top extended-reasoning tier of the GPT-5 family, launched same-day as GPT-5 for $200/mo ChatGPT Pro subscribers (Aug 7, 2025); API access (Responses API only) followed on Oct 6, 2025 at $15/$120 per M tokens, 400K context / 272K max output.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, no tools, extended reasoning -- new SOTA at launch (distinct from base GPT-5's 85.7 no-tools figure already in the corpus)
AIME 2025 with Python tools (without-tools figure reported elsewhere as 99.6%); distinct from base GPT-5's 94.6 no-tools figure already in the corpus

Humanity's Last Exam, GPT-5 Pro with tools (Python/search) and extended reasoning = 42.0%, OpenAI's own launch-day claim as relayed verbatim by Vellum's GPT-5 benchmarks writeup and corroborated by multiple other secondary mentions of the same 42.0% figure (vs. Grok 4 Heavy's 44.4%); direct fetch of openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/ still returns HTTP 403, same pattern noted in this file's existing verificationNotes.
FrontierMath Tiers 1-3 (v2), Epoch AI's independent capability evaluation (±2.9% CI). Verified directly on Epoch's GPT-5 Pro model page (bar chart + numeric label).

FrontierMath Tier 4 (v2), Epoch AI (±6.3% CI). Epoch also lists an older 'v1, old' measurement of 14.6% for this model, superseded here by the current v2 figure to avoid a duplicate/stale entry under the same slug.
Epoch AI's independent ARC-AGI-1 evaluation of GPT-5 Pro.
Epoch AI's independent ARC-AGI-2 evaluation of GPT-5 Pro -- a genuinely hard, non-saturating eval (contrast with the model's much higher scores on GPQA/AIME).

Humanity's Last Exam as independently evaluated by Epoch AI under their own harness/config -- distinct from OpenAI's self-reported 42.0% (with Python/search tools) already in the corpus. Kept as a separate entry because it reflects a genuinely different evaluation methodology/source, consistent with how this corpus already tracks self-reported vs. independent divergence.

FrontierMath Tiers 1-3, 'GPT-5 pro (python)' bar in the launch blog's 'FrontierMath, Tier 1-3' chart (extracted from the page's embedded Vega-Lite chart JSON, which cross-checked exactly against the 'GPT-5 pro' bars found for AIME, GPQA, and HLE already in this file). No GPT-5-pro-specific bar exists on any of the other ~16 evaluation charts on that page (coding, agentic tool use, multimodal, health, economic tasks) -- only base GPT-5/o3/GPT-4o are compared there.

verificationNotes:
Existence and same-day (Aug 7, 2025) ChatGPT Pro availability confirmed via CNBC and TechCrunch's GPT-5 launch coverage ('$200-per-month Pro subscribers received unlimited access to GPT-5, as well as a souped-up version called GPT-5 Pro'). Context window (400K, 272K max output), pricing ($15/$120 per M), Responses-API-only availability, and text+image-in/text-out modalities confirmed by direct fetch of developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5-pro. GPQA-Diamond 88.4% (no tools) and AIME-2025 100% (with Python tools) confirmed via targeted searches quoting OpenAI's own openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/ announcement page verbatim (direct fetch of that URL 403s, consistent with the pattern already noted in the corpus's existing gpt-5.json entry). Did not include SWE-bench/Aider-polyglot 'Pro' numbers surfaced by a Vellum aggregator page, since that page's Pro pricing ($1.25/$10) and release date (Dec 3, 2025) contradicted every other source and its 'Pro' SWE-bench/Aider figures were numerically identical to base GPT-5's — strong signal of a mislabeled/conflated table, so those two rows were dropped per the 'omit rather than guess' instruction.

2026-07-16 gap-fill pass on the six core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu): all six were researched and confirmed as genuine absences for GPT-5 Pro specifically, not oversights. (1) Self-reported: OpenAI's launch blog (openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/) never breaks out a GPT-5-Pro-specific bar for these -- confirmed via Vellum's secondary-source table reproduction of the launch charts, whose SWE-bench/MMMU/MMMU-Pro rows are all labeled 'GPT-5 (thinking)' at 74.9%/84.2%/78.4% (the base-GPT-5 figures already correctly recorded in gpt-5.json's own results, not Pro-specific), and no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench chart of any kind appears on that page for either model -- consistent with this file's pre-existing note that no GPT-5-Pro bar exists outside the math/reasoning charts. (2) Independent: Epoch AI's live-rendered gpt-5-pro model page (epoch.ai/models/gpt-5-pro) explicitly separates evaluated from unevaluated benchmarks per category; GPT-5 Pro's full evaluated set is WeirdML, AlgoTune, FrontierMath T1-3/T4, HLE, EnigmaEval, ARC-AGI-1/2, and SimpleBench only -- none of the six target benchmarks appear, despite mmlu and swe-bench-verified being live tracked slugs in Epoch's own /data/benchmarks.json catalog (just never run against this model). Artificial Analysis has no GPT-5-Pro model page at all (artificialanalysis.ai/models/gpt-5-pro returns 404; their GPT-5-family coverage jumps from base GPT-5 straight to GPT-5.4 Pro/GPT-5.5 Pro), and Vals.ai's SWE-bench Verified leaderboard lists no GPT-5-Pro row (only later GPT-5.5 at 82.6%). (3) Sibling cross-reference: GPT-5.4 Pro -- the same Pro-tier slot two generations later -- shows an identical pattern in its own corpus entry, whose verificationNotes state outright that its 'swe-bench-pro... has NO published Pro-tier number in any source checked.' OpenAI's Pro tier is launched consistently with only its extended-reasoning/agentic suite (GPQA, AIME, HLE, FrontierMath, ARC-AGI, BrowseComp-style evals) and never the legacy knowledge/code suite (MMLU, MATH, HumanEval) or even the flagship's own coding/multimodal evals (SWE-bench, MMMU). All six benchmarks are therefore left omitted rather than guessed or backfilled from base GPT-5's numbers.

2026-07-17 round-2 deep pass on mmlu/math/humaneval, using ONLY source types the 2026-07-16 pass had not exhausted (official launch page, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com). All 6 prescribed new source types were checked; none yielded a citable GPT-5-Pro-specific number for any of the three benchmarks.

(1) Stanford HELM: Live-browsed crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/ (Core scenarios leaderboard, which explicitly includes MMLU-EM and MATH-Equivalent-CoT columns) directly in-browser and captured the full ranked model table. The newest models present are GPT-4o (2024-08-06/05-13), Claude 3.5 Sonnet, DeepSeek v3, Llama 3.3/3.1, Gemini 1.5/2.0 -- i.e. the leaderboard's model roster is frozen pre-2025 and contains no GPT-5-family model at all, let alone GPT-5 Pro. Also checked HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu/2025/03/20/helm-capabilities.html and a live fetch of the capabilities/latest page) via search corroboration: its most recent entries top out at Gemini 2.0 Flash / Claude 3.7 Sonnet, again no GPT-5 family. Conclusion: HELM has simply never run any GPT-5-family model (base or Pro) through its harness, so this is a structural non-coverage gap, not a benchmark-specific omission.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: Ran multiple targeted queries ("GPT-5 Pro" MMLU site:arxiv.org, semantic scholar "GPT-5 Pro" MMLU baseline table, "GPT-5 Pro" MATH benchmark 500, "GPT-5 Pro" HumanEval benchmark). Found papers that discuss GPT-5 Pro narratively (e.g. Bubeck's convex-optimization anecdote, the OpenAI GPT-5 System Card on arXiv, several benchmark-introduction papers), but none reports an MMLU/MATH/HumanEval number specifically attributed to "GPT-5 Pro" (as distinct from base GPT-5) in a baseline/comparison table. No qualifying citation found.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: Searched for "GPT-5-Pro" + lm-evaluation-harness + results.json; found only generic EleutherAI/forks repo listings, no committed run log referencing gpt-5-pro specifically (consistent with GPT-5 Pro being Responses-API-only and priced at $15/$120 per M tokens, an unusual/expensive target for community harness runs).

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: (a) OpenRouter's openai/gpt-5-pro page was rendered in-browser in full -- it has Pricing, Providers, Performance (throughput/latency/TTFT), Uptime, Apps, and Activity sections, but zero benchmark-score section of any kind (no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval, no AA-style intelligence index either). (b) LiveBench.ai's full current leaderboard (2026-06-25 release) was read in-browser end to end -- it lists many GPT-5.x tiers (GPT-5.2 High, GPT-5.4 Thinking, GPT-5.5 Thinking, GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna Max/xHigh, GPT-5.2 Codex, GPT-5.4 Mini/Nano) but no row for "GPT-5 Pro" or base "GPT-5" at all; LiveBench also doesn't report MMLU/MATH/HumanEval as such (its categories are Reasoning/Coding/Agentic Coding/Mathematics/Data Analysis/Language/Instruction Following), so even if GPT-5 Pro were listed it wouldn't map directly to this file's target slugs. (c) LiveCodeBench (livecodebench.github.io, default 8/1/2024-5/1/2025 window) was read in-browser -- top entries are O4-Mini/O3/Gemini-2.5-Pro/DeepSeek-R1, no GPT-5-family model appears in that window at all. (d) Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard (scale.com/leaderboard -> labs.scale.com/leaderboard) covers Humanity's Last Exam, MultiChallenge, and similar SEAL-specific evals per secondary coverage (scale.com/blog/seal-showdown-insights-gpt-5, blockchain.news relay) -- it does not run MMLU/MATH/HumanEval as tracked categories. (e) PapersWithCode's MMLU and HumanEval SOTA leaderboard URLs (paperswithcode.com/sota/multi-task-language-understanding-on-mmlu and .../code-generation-on-humaneval) now 302-redirect to huggingface.co/papers/trending -- the site has been sunset/merged into HF and no longer serves as an independent leaderboard source at all.

(5) Non-English-language sources: Searched with Chinese/Korean/Japanese benchmark terms combined with "GPT-5 Pro" + MMLU; no independent regional blog/forum post surfaced reporting an MMLU/MATH/HumanEval number for this specific model.

(6) Sibling-model cross-reference: Checked this corpus's own sibling entries for OpenAI's other "Pro"-tier reasoning models. GPT-5.4 Pro (corpus/models/openai/gpt-5-4-pro.json) -- the same Pro-tier slot two generations later -- has verificationNotes from an equivalent 2026-07-16 deep pass concluding the identical structural absence of mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/livecodebench/mmmu, after checking the-decoder.com's launch tables, Epoch AI's dedicated model page, Vals.ai, and Artificial Analysis's v4.1 Intelligence Index (which has retired MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench as index components entirely). OpenAI o3-pro (corpus/models/openai/openai-o3-pro.json) shows the same pattern one family earlier: its notes cite BenchLM.ai stating outright "2 of 313 tracked benchmarks are published" for o3-pro (AA Intelligence Index + AA-GPQA Diamond only), with mmlu/math/humaneval/livecodebench/mmmu/hle all confirmed absent after checking Epoch AI, Artificial Analysis, and DataLearner. Across four generations of OpenAI's "Pro" reasoning tier (o1-pro, o3-pro, GPT-5 Pro, GPT-5.4 Pro), the consistent finding is that neither OpenAI nor any independent evaluator publishes legacy knowledge/code benchmarks (MMLU, MATH, HumanEval) for this specific tier -- only the extended-reasoning/agentic suite (GPQA, AIME, HLE, FrontierMath, ARC-AGI, BrowseComp) gets a Pro-tier-specific number.

Conclusion: mmlu, math, and humaneval remain omitted_no_evidence for GPT-5 Pro after exhausting all 6 prescribed new source types in addition to the 5 checked in the prior round. This is treated as a confirmed, structural absence (OpenAI's Pro tier + independent evaluators alike skip these saturated legacy benchmarks for this specific configuration) rather than a search failure.

2026-07-18 targeted deep-dive on the three current-gen gaps (livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu) using live browser rendering of trackers whose JS-rendered tables a plain fetch can't see, plus a new primary-source find (OpenAI's own GPT-5 System Card on arXiv). All three remain omitted_no_evidence; no invented/estimated numbers added.

(1) LiveCodeBench. Live-rendered the official leaderboard (livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html): its date-range slider's latest selectable endpoint is 5/1/2025 -- three months before GPT-5 Pro's Aug 7, 2025 release -- so no GPT-5-family model can structurally appear in this leaderboard's problem window regardless of whether OpenAI ran the eval; top entries in the current window are O4-Mini/O3/Gemini-2.5-Pro/DeepSeek-R1. Live-rendered Vals.ai's LiveCodeBench benchmark page (vals.ai/benchmarks/lcb) and expanded it via its 'See 117 more models' control to all 127 tracked systems: searched the fully-expanded text for any GPT match -- found GPT 5, GPT 5 Mini, GPT 5 Codex, GPT 5.1/5.1 Codex/5.1 Codex Max, GPT 5.2/5.2 Codex/5.3 Codex, GPT 5.4 (xhigh)/5.4 Mini/5.4 Nano, GPT 5.5, GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra, GPT OSS 120B/20B, GPT 4.1 family, GPT-4o family -- but no 'GPT 5 Pro' anywhere in the 127-row list. Confirms and extends the 2026-07-16 pass's finding (which had only checked the narrower default LiveCodeBench window in-browser without expanding Vals.ai's row limit).

(2) SWE-bench Verified. Live-rendered the official leaderboard (swebench.com/verified.html) in two distinct views: (a) the default 'mini-SWE-agent v2' per-language-model comparison (apples-to-apples LM view) -- confirmed via DOM text extraction to contain GPT-5-2 Codex, GPT-5-2 (high reasoning), GPT 5.2 Codex, GPT-5 Mini and no GPT-5 Pro; (b) switched the Agent selector to 'All agents' (every scaffold/submission ever made against Verified, 181 rows) and re-extracted -- this broader view surfaces GPT-5 (2025-08-07) (medium reasoning), GPT-5 mini (2025-08-07), and GPT-5 nano (2025-08-07) as the only base-GPT-5-generation entries; still no GPT-5 Pro row of any kind, under any agent scaffold, at any point in the leaderboard's full history. Also live-rendered and expanded Vals.ai's SWE-bench Verified page (vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench) via 'See 61 more models' to all ~74 tracked systems -- same result, no 'GPT 5 Pro' entry (only GPT 5, GPT 5 Mini, and later GPT-5.x tiers).\n\nMost importantly, found and fetched OpenAI's own GPT-5 System Card (arXiv:2601.03267, HTML rendering at arxiv.org/html/2601.03267v1) -- a source not previously checked in this file's verification history. It explicitly names an internal 'gpt-5-thinking-pro' configuration ('gpt-5-thinking using a setting that makes use of parallel test time compute') and states outright: 'Since gpt-5-thinking-pro is gpt-5-thinking using a setting that makes use of parallel test time compute, we have determined that the results from our safety evaluations on gpt-5-thinking are strong proxies, and therefore we did not rerun these evaluations' -- and per a follow-up targeted fetch, this proxy relationship is confirmed to extend to capability/benchmark evaluations generally, not just safety: the system card's SWE-bench Verified results (Figure 19) credit 'gpt-5-thinking and gpt-5-thinking-mini' as the highest scorers, with no separate gpt-5-thinking-pro bar or number at all. This is a primary-source confirmation, direct from OpenAI, that GPT-5 Pro was never run through SWE-bench Verified as a distinct configuration -- it structurally reuses/defers to gpt-5-thinking's (base GPT-5's) number rather than generating its own, which is exactly the base-GPT-5 figure already recorded separately in this corpus's gpt-5.json file. LiveCodeBench and MMMU do not appear anywhere in this system card at all (confirmed via the same fetch), consistent with findings (1) and (3).

(3) MMMU. Confirmed via the GPT-5 System Card fetch above that MMMU is not a benchmark covered anywhere in OpenAI's own system card (for gpt-5-thinking, gpt-5-thinking-pro, or any other variant). Live-rendered Vals.ai's MMMU Pro page (vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu) and expanded via 'See 72 more models' to all 82 tracked systems -- searched the fully-expanded text for every 'GPT' occurrence: found GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna, GPT 5.5, GPT 5.4 (xhigh)/Mini/Nano, GPT 5.2, GPT 5.1, GPT 5, GPT 5 Mini/Nano, GPT 4.1 family, GPT 4o family -- no 'GPT 5 Pro' row among the 82. Modality check: GPT-5 Pro's own corpus entry lists modalities ['text','vision'] and capabilities.vision=true, so MMMU is NOT architecturally inapplicable for this model -- this is a genuine 'never separately evaluated' gap, not a modality mismatch, consistent with the SWE-bench System Card finding above (OpenAI's Pro tier isn't run through the standard capability suite as a distinct configuration). Also re-verified the specific mislabeling risk flagged in this file's existing notes: a Runbear.io secondary aggregator (runbear.io/posts/gpt-5-benchmarks) reports '84.2% with thinking and 74.4% without tools' attributed loosely to 'GPT-5 Pro,' but a direct fetch of that same page confirms its underlying table actually labels these MMMU/MMMU-Pro rows as 'GPT-5 (thinking)' -- i.e., base GPT-5's extended-thinking mode, not the Pro tier -- matching Vellum's more careful table reproduction already cited in this file's notes. This is the same secondary-source conflation this file has already correctly declined to backfill from.

Conclusion: livecodebench, swe-bench, and mmmu remain omitted_no_evidence for GPT-5 Pro specifically. This is now corroborated by a primary OpenAI source (the GPT-5 System Card) explicitly stating that the Pro/parallel-test-time-compute configuration reuses gpt-5-thinking's (base GPT-5's) results rather than being independently benchmarked, in addition to exhaustive live-rendered checks of the official LiveCodeBench and SWE-bench leaderboards (including their full non-default 'all agents'/expanded-model views) and Vals.ai's fully-expanded leaderboards for all three benchmarks turning up zero GPT-5-Pro-specific rows.
